holidays.funFact: Social Media Day was first celebrated on June 30, 2010, and there are now more than 5 billion social media users worldwide.
holidays.shareText: Connecting the world one post at a time on Social Media Day! 📱 #SocialMediaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Canada Day
holidays.description: Canada Day celebrates the anniversary of Canadian Confederation on July 1, 1867, when Canada became a self governing dominion.
holidays.funFact: Canada Day was originally called Dominion Day until 1982 when it was officially renamed Canada Day.
holidays.shareText: Happy Canada Day! Celebrating the Great White North today! 🍁 #CanadaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Gingersnap Day
holidays.description: National Gingersnap Day honors the crispy, spicy ginger flavored cookie beloved by snack lovers everywhere.
holidays.funFact: Gingersnaps get their name from their signature snap when broken, a result of their crispy baked texture.
holidays.shareText: Snapping into National Gingersnap Day with the crunchiest cookie around! 🍪 #NationalGingersnapDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World UFO Day
holidays.description: World UFO Day encourages people to look up at the sky and ponder the possibility of extraterrestrial life and unidentified flying objects.
holidays.funFact: The term UFO was coined by the U.S. Air Force in 1952 to describe any airborne object that could not be immediately identified.
holidays.shareText: The truth is out there! Happy World UFO Day! 🛸 #WorldUFODay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Anisette Day
holidays.description: National Anisette Day celebrates the sweet anise flavored liqueur enjoyed in cocktails and desserts around the world.
holidays.funFact: Anisette has been produced for centuries and was historically used as a medicinal remedy for digestive ailments.
holidays.shareText: Raising a glass to the sweet licorice taste of anisette today! 🥃 #NationalAnisetteDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Chocolate Wafer Day
holidays.description: National Chocolate Wafer Day celebrates the thin, crispy chocolate wafer cookie that is a beloved snack and dessert ingredient.
holidays.funFact: Chocolate wafers are a key ingredient in many classic no bake desserts, including the famous icebox cake.
holidays.shareText: Crunching into National Chocolate Wafer Day with a stack of sweet, crispy goodness! 🍫 #NationalChocolateWaferDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Stay Out of the Sun Day
holidays.description: Stay Out of the Sun Day reminds us to protect our skin during the hottest part of summer by staying in the shade.
holidays.funFact: UV radiation is strongest between 10 a.m. and 4 p.m., making midday the most important time to seek shade.
holidays.shareText: Staying cool and protected today for Stay Out of the Sun Day! 🌂 #StayOutOfTheSunDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Independence Day
holidays.description: Independence Day commemorates the adoption of the Declaration of Independence on July 4, 1776, marking the birth of the United States of America.
holidays.funFact: The tradition of fireworks on the Fourth of July dates back to the very first celebration in Philadelphia in 1777.
holidays.shareText: Happy Fourth of July! Celebrating freedom and fireworks today! 🎆 #IndependenceDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Barbecue Day
holidays.description: National Barbecue Day celebrates the beloved American tradition of grilling food outdoors with family and friends.
holidays.funFact: The word barbecue is believed to derive from the Caribbean Taino word barabicu, meaning sacred fire pit.
holidays.shareText: Firing up the grill for National Barbecue Day! Nothing says summer like BBQ! 🍖 #NationalBarbecueDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Apple Turnover Day
holidays.description: National Apple Turnover Day celebrates the delicious pastry filled with spiced apple filling and wrapped in flaky dough.
holidays.funFact: Apple turnovers are believed to have originated in England and became popular in the American colonies in the 1700s.
holidays.shareText: Turning over a new (apple) leaf for National Apple Turnover Day! 🥐 #NationalAppleTurnoverDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Workaholics Day
holidays.description: Workaholics Day is a humorous holiday that acknowledges those who are dedicated to their work, perhaps a little too much.
holidays.funFact: The term workaholic was coined in 1971 by psychologist Wayne Oates in his book Confessions of a Workaholic.
holidays.shareText: Taking a break from being a workaholic to celebrate Workaholics Day! 💼 #WorkaholicsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Fried Chicken Day
holidays.description: National Fried Chicken Day celebrates one of America's most beloved comfort foods, golden, crispy, and delicious.
holidays.funFact: The American South became famous for fried chicken after Scottish immigrants brought their frying techniques and enslaved African cooks added their own seasonings and spices.
holidays.shareText: It is National Fried Chicken Day and I am absolutely here for it! 🍗 #NationalFriedChickenDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Kissing Day
holidays.description: International Kissing Day celebrates the simple yet meaningful act of kissing and the joy it brings to relationships.
holidays.funFact: Kissing is thought to have evolved from kiss feeding, the practice of mothers passing chewed food to their infants.
holidays.shareText: Spreading love and kisses on International Kissing Day! 💋 #InternationalKissingDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Chocolate Day
holidays.description: World Chocolate Day is a global celebration of one of humanity's most beloved treats, enjoyed in countless forms around the world.
holidays.funFact: It takes approximately 400 cocoa beans to make one pound of chocolate, and each cacao tree produces only about 2,500 beans per year.
holidays.shareText: Every day should be World Chocolate Day, but today it is official! 🍫 #WorldChocolateDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Strawberry Sundae Day
holidays.description: National Strawberry Sundae Day celebrates the classic ice cream sundae topped with fresh strawberries and whipped cream.
holidays.funFact: The ice cream sundae was invented in the late 1800s when laws in some U.S. towns prohibited selling soda water on Sundays, leading to sodaless ice cream dishes.
holidays.shareText: Topping off my day with a strawberry sundae! 🍓 #NationalStrawberrySundaeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Milk Chocolate with Almonds Day
holidays.description: National Milk Chocolate with Almonds Day celebrates the perfect combination of creamy milk chocolate and crunchy roasted almonds.
holidays.funFact: Almonds are one of the oldest and most widely cultivated nut crops, with evidence of almond cultivation dating back over 5,000 years.
holidays.shareText: The best combo ever gets its own day! Happy National Milk Chocolate with Almonds Day! 🍫 #MilkChocolateWithAlmondsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Video Games Day
holidays.description: Video Games Day celebrates the art of video gaming and the countless hours of joy, challenge, and adventure games have brought to people worldwide.
holidays.funFact: The first commercially sold video game was Computer Space in 1971, though Pong in 1972 became the first commercially successful arcade game.
holidays.shareText: Player one has entered the game! Happy Video Games Day! 🎮 #VideoGamesDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Sugar Cookie Day
holidays.description: National Sugar Cookie Day celebrates the simple, sweet, and endlessly versatile sugar cookie loved by bakers and dessert fans of all ages.
holidays.funFact: Sugar cookies originated in the mid 1700s in Nazareth, Pennsylvania, where German Protestant settlers baked them in the shape of the keystone state symbol.
holidays.shareText: Sweetening up my day with sugar cookies for National Sugar Cookie Day! 🍪 #NationalSugarCookieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Disco Day
holidays.description: Disco Day celebrates the vibrant music genre and cultural movement that dominated the 1970s dance floors around the world.
holidays.funFact: Disco music gets its name from discotheque, the French word for a dance club or nightclub that played recorded music.
holidays.shareText: Stayin alive and dancing all night for Disco Day! 🪩 #DiscoDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pina Colada Day
holidays.description: National Pina Colada Day celebrates the iconic tropical cocktail made with rum, coconut cream, and pineapple juice.
holidays.funFact: The pina colada is the official drink of Puerto Rico and was reportedly invented at the Caribe Hilton hotel in San Juan in 1954.
holidays.shareText: If you like pina coladas, today is YOUR day! 🍹 #NationalPinaColadaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Clerihew Day
holidays.description: Clerihew Day celebrates the clerihew, a whimsical four line biographical poem with a specific rhyme scheme invented by Edmund Clerihew Bentley.
holidays.funFact: Edmund Clerihew Bentley invented the clerihew form at age 16 while bored in a science class, writing his first poem about Sir Humphry Davy.
holidays.shareText: Writing silly little poems about famous people for Clerihew Day! 📝 #ClerihewDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Population Day
holidays.description: World Population Day is a United Nations observance that highlights the urgency and importance of population issues around the globe.
holidays.funFact: World Population Day was established in 1989 after world population reached five billion people on July 11, 1987, a date known as Five Billion Day.
holidays.shareText: Thinking globally on World Population Day and reflecting on our shared humanity. 🌍 #WorldPopulationDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Blueberry Muffin Day
holidays.description: National Blueberry Muffin Day celebrates one of the most popular muffin flavors, bursting with fresh blueberries in every bite.
holidays.funFact: The muffin tin was invented in the early 1800s, but blueberry muffins became especially popular in the United States after blueberries were successfully cultivated commercially in the early 1900s.
holidays.shareText: Starting the morning right with a fresh blueberry muffin! 🫐 #NationalBlueberryMuffinDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pecan Pie Day
holidays.description: National Pecan Pie Day celebrates the rich, sweet Southern classic made with pecans, corn syrup, and a buttery crust.
holidays.funFact: Pecans are the only major tree nut native to North America, and the pecan pie recipe first appeared in print in 1898.
holidays.shareText: A slice of heaven for National Pecan Pie Day! 🥧 #NationalPecanPieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Different Colored Eyes Day
holidays.description: Different Colored Eyes Day celebrates heterochromia, the fascinating genetic condition where a person has two different colored eyes.
holidays.funFact: Heterochromia affects less than one percent of the human population, but is much more common in animals like huskies and cats.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the rare beauty of two different colored eyes today! 👁️ #DifferentColoredEyesDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National French Fries Day
holidays.description: National French Fries Day celebrates one of the world's most beloved side dishes, golden and crispy potato sticks enjoyed in every corner of the globe.
holidays.funFact: Despite their name, french fries are believed to have originated in Belgium, where villagers near the Meuse River were frying fish and turned to frying potatoes during winter when the river froze.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the most iconic side dish on National French Fries Day! 🍟 #NationalFrenchFriesDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Gruntled Workers Day
holidays.description: Gruntled Workers Day is a fun holiday that celebrates satisfied, happy, and content employees everywhere, playing on the word disgruntled.
holidays.funFact: The word gruntled actually is a legitimate English word meaning pleased or satisfied, making it the positive opposite of disgruntled.
holidays.shareText: Feeling fully gruntled at work today! Happy Gruntled Workers Day! 😊 #GruntledWorkersDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Bastille Day
holidays.description: Bastille Day is the French national holiday commemorating the storming of the Bastille prison in 1789, a pivotal event in the French Revolution.
holidays.funFact: When the Bastille was stormed on July 14, 1789, only seven prisoners were actually found inside, but the event became a powerful symbol of the revolution.
holidays.shareText: Vive la France! Celebrating Bastille Day and the spirit of liberte, egalite, fraternite! 🇫🇷 #BastilleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Grand Marnier Day
holidays.description: National Grand Marnier Day celebrates the iconic French orange liqueur made from a blend of Cognac brandy and distilled essence of bitter orange.
holidays.funFact: Grand Marnier was created in 1880 by Alexandre Marnier Lapostolle and named by his friend Cesar Ritz, the famous hotelier.
holidays.shareText: Raising a glass of something grand on National Grand Marnier Day! 🍊 #NationalGrandMarnierDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Gummy Worm Day
holidays.description: National Gummy Worm Day celebrates the wiggly, chewy, colorful candy worms that have been a childhood favorite for generations.
holidays.funFact: Gummy worms were invented by the German candy company Trolli in 1981, building on the earlier success of gummy bears invented by Hans Riegel in 1922.
holidays.shareText: Getting wiggly with it for National Gummy Worm Day! 🐛 #NationalGummyWormDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Give Something Away Day
holidays.description: Give Something Away Day encourages generosity and the joy of sharing by giving away something meaningful to someone else.
holidays.funFact: Studies show that giving to others activates the same reward centers in the brain as receiving gifts, producing what scientists call a helper's high.
holidays.shareText: Spreading joy today by giving something away! It feels amazing! 🎁 #GiveSomethingAwayDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Fresh Spinach Day
holidays.description: National Fresh Spinach Day celebrates the leafy green vegetable packed with iron, vitamins, and nutrients that make it a superfood.
holidays.funFact: Spinach is one of the most nutrient dense foods on earth and was used by the French to create a green coloring for pasta and other foods.
holidays.shareText: Eating my greens and feeling strong on National Fresh Spinach Day! 🥬 #NationalFreshSpinachDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Guinea Pig Appreciation Day
holidays.description: Guinea Pig Appreciation Day celebrates the adorable and sociable small pets that have been beloved companions for centuries.
holidays.funFact: Guinea pigs are not pigs and are not from Guinea. They are rodents native to the Andes of South America and were domesticated around 5000 BCE.
holidays.shareText: Showering these adorable little fluffballs with love on Guinea Pig Appreciation Day! 🐹 #GuineaPigAppreciationDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Peach Ice Cream Day
holidays.description: National Peach Ice Cream Day celebrates the creamy, fruity frozen treat made with fresh peaches that is the taste of summer.
holidays.funFact: Georgia is famously known as the Peach State, but California actually produces the most peaches in the United States.
holidays.shareText: Nothing says summer like a scoop of peach ice cream! 🍑 #NationalPeachIceCreamDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Emoji Day
holidays.description: World Emoji Day is an annual celebration of emoji and their impact on digital communication, observed on the date shown on the calendar emoji.
holidays.funFact: The calendar emoji on most platforms shows July 17, which is why that date was chosen for World Emoji Day when it was created in 2014.
holidays.shareText: Expressing all the feelings with emojis on World Emoji Day! 😀🎉🌟 #WorldEmojiDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Caviar Day
holidays.description: National Caviar Day celebrates the luxurious delicacy of salt cured fish roe that has been considered a gourmet treat for centuries.
holidays.funFact: Beluga caviar is the most expensive food in the world by weight, with some varieties selling for over $35,000 per kilogram.
holidays.shareText: Living the fancy life for National Caviar Day! 🐟 #NationalCaviarDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Nelson Mandela International Day
holidays.description: Nelson Mandela International Day honors the life and legacy of the South African anti apartheid leader and Nobel Peace Prize winner.
holidays.funFact: The United Nations designated July 18 as Nelson Mandela International Day in 2009, on what would have been his 91st birthday, calling for 67 minutes of community service.
holidays.shareText: Honoring the incredible legacy of Nelson Mandela today by making a difference. ✊ #MandelaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Daiquiri Day
holidays.description: National Daiquiri Day celebrates the classic rum based cocktail made with lime juice and sugar that is a staple of tropical summer drinking.
holidays.funFact: The daiquiri is named after Daiquiri, a beach and iron mining town in Cuba, where American engineer Jennings Cox invented the drink in 1898.
holidays.shareText: Sipping on something icy and refreshing for National Daiquiri Day! 🍹 #NationalDaiquiriDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Flitch Day
holidays.description: Flitch Day is an ancient tradition from Dunmow, England, where a couple proves they have not quarreled for a year and a day to win a side of bacon.
holidays.funFact: The Dunmow Flitch Trials date back to at least 1104 and still take place every four years in Great Dunmow, Essex, England.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating wedded bliss and free bacon on Flitch Day! 🥓 #FlitchDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Moon Day
holidays.description: National Moon Day commemorates the first time humans walked on the moon on July 20, 1969, when Apollo 11 astronauts Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin landed on the lunar surface.
holidays.funFact: Neil Armstrong's famous first words on the moon were: That is one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind, though he intended to say a man.
holidays.shareText: One giant leap for mankind happened on this day! Happy National Moon Day! 🌕 #NationalMoonDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Hot Dog Day
holidays.description: National Hot Dog Day celebrates the iconic American food that is a staple of ballparks, backyard cookouts, and street vendors across the country.
holidays.funFact: Americans consume an estimated 20 billion hot dogs per year, and baseball stadiums alone serve approximately 19 million hot dogs each season.
holidays.shareText: It is National Hot Dog Day and I am absolutely relishing it! 🌭 #NationalHotDogDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Junk Food Day
holidays.description: National Junk Food Day gives everyone permission to indulge in their favorite guilty pleasure snacks and treats without judgment.
holidays.funFact: The term junk food was coined by consumer advocate Michael Jacobson of the Center for Science in the Public Interest in 1972.
holidays.shareText: Giving myself full permission to indulge on National Junk Food Day! 🍕 #NationalJunkFoodDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Creme Brulee Day
holidays.description: National Creme Brulee Day celebrates the elegant French dessert of rich custard topped with a layer of hardened caramelized sugar.
holidays.funFact: The earliest known recipe for creme brulee appeared in a French cookbook in 1691, and the dessert name means burnt cream in French.
holidays.shareText: Cracking into the perfect caramelized top for National Creme Brulee Day! 🍮 #NationalCremeBruleeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Mango Day
holidays.description: National Mango Day celebrates the king of fruits, the tropical mango, beloved worldwide for its sweet, juicy, and aromatic flesh.
holidays.funFact: Mangoes have been cultivated in South Asia for over 4,000 years and are the national fruit of India, Pakistan, and the Philippines.
holidays.shareText: The king of fruits deserves its own day! Happy National Mango Day! 🥭 #NationalMangoDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Hammock Day
holidays.description: National Hammock Day celebrates the simple pleasure of relaxing in a hammock on a warm summer day, swaying gently in the breeze.
holidays.funFact: The hammock was invented by the indigenous peoples of Central and South America and was adopted by European sailors in the 15th century because it kept them off the damp ship floors.
holidays.shareText: Swinging into relaxation mode for National Hammock Day! 🏖️ #NationalHammockDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Vanilla Ice Cream Day
holidays.description: National Vanilla Ice Cream Day celebrates the classic and most popular ice cream flavor in the world, beloved for its creamy simplicity.
holidays.funFact: Vanilla is actually the second most expensive spice in the world after saffron because vanilla orchid flowers must be hand pollinated and the beans take months to cure.
holidays.shareText: Classic, creamy, and absolutely perfect! Happy National Vanilla Ice Cream Day! 🍦 #NationalVanillaIceCreamDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Hot Enough for Ya Day
holidays.description: Hot Enough for Ya Day is a humorous holiday acknowledging the peak of summer heat and the universal conversation about how hot it is.
holidays.funFact: The phrase hot enough for ya is one of the most commonly used small talk expressions in the United States during summer months.
holidays.shareText: Is it hot enough for ya? Because today is the official day to complain about summer heat! 🌡️ #HotEnoughForYaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Drive Thru Day
holidays.description: National Drive Thru Day celebrates the convenience of drive through restaurants, a uniquely American invention that changed fast food culture.
holidays.funFact: The first drive through restaurant is generally credited to Red's Giant Hamburg in Springfield, Missouri, which opened in 1947, though In N Out Burger claims to have opened the first intercom based drive through in 1948.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the greatest invention in fast food history on National Drive Thru Day! 🚗 #NationalDriveThruDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Tequila Day
holidays.description: National Tequila Day celebrates Mexico's most iconic spirit, distilled from the blue agave plant in the region surrounding the city of Tequila.
holidays.funFact: Tequila can only legally be produced in specific regions of Mexico, and the blue agave plant used to make it takes 8 to 12 years to mature before it can be harvested.
holidays.shareText: Salt, lime, and good times for National Tequila Day! 🥃 #NationalTequilaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Culinarians Day
holidays.description: National Culinarians Day honors professional chefs, cooks, and all culinary artists who bring creativity and skill to the kitchen.
holidays.funFact: The word culinarian comes from the Latin word culina, meaning kitchen, and the profession of chef dates back to ancient Rome.
holidays.shareText: Raising a spatula to the talented culinary artists who feed the world! 👨‍🍳 #NationalCulinariansDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Thread the Needle Day
holidays.description: Thread the Needle Day celebrates the art of sewing, needlework, and embroidery, honoring crafters and seamstresses who create with thread.
holidays.funFact: The oldest known sewing needles, carved from bone, date back approximately 50,000 years, making sewing one of humanity's oldest crafts.
holidays.shareText: Getting crafty and creative on Thread the Needle Day! 🧵 #ThreadTheNeedleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Coffee Milkshake Day
holidays.description: National Coffee Milkshake Day celebrates the delightful blend of creamy ice cream and rich coffee flavor in one indulgent frozen drink.
holidays.funFact: Coffee milkshakes are sometimes called frappes in New England and coffee cabinets in Rhode Island, showcasing America's rich regional food culture.
holidays.shareText: The best of both worlds in one glass! Happy National Coffee Milkshake Day! ☕ #NationalCoffeeMilkshakeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Americans with Disabilities Act Day
holidays.description: Americans with Disabilities Act Day marks the signing of the ADA on July 26, 1990, a landmark civil rights law prohibiting discrimination based on disability.
holidays.funFact: The Americans with Disabilities Act was signed by President George H.W. Bush and covers employment, public services, public accommodations, and telecommunications for over 61 million Americans living with a disability.
holidays.shareText: Honoring the rights and dignity of all people on Americans with Disabilities Act Day. ♿ #ADADay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Scotch Day
holidays.description: National Scotch Day celebrates Scotch whisky, the iconic distilled spirit from Scotland that has been crafted for centuries using traditional methods.
holidays.funFact: To be called Scotch, whisky must be distilled and matured in Scotland for a minimum of three years in oak casks, and single malt Scotch must come from a single distillery.
holidays.shareText: Savoring the smoky, complex flavors of a fine Scotch on National Scotch Day! 🥃 #NationalScotchDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Walk on Stilts Day
holidays.description: Walk on Stilts Day is a fun, whimsical holiday encouraging people to try the ancient art of stilt walking and see the world from a new height.
holidays.funFact: Stilts have been used for centuries by fishermen in parts of France and Southeast Asia to walk into shallow water to fish, making them both practical and impressive.
holidays.shareText: Rising above the ordinary on Walk on Stilts Day! 🎪 #WalkOnStiltsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Milk Chocolate Day
holidays.description: National Milk Chocolate Day celebrates the smooth, creamy, and sweet variety of chocolate made with milk solids that is the world's most popular confection.
holidays.funFact: Milk chocolate was invented in 1875 by Swiss chocolatier Daniel Peter, who partnered with his neighbor Henri Nestle to develop a method for adding condensed milk to chocolate.
holidays.shareText: Melting for the creamy sweetness of milk chocolate on National Milk Chocolate Day! 🍫 #NationalMilkChocolateDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Hepatitis Day
holidays.description: World Hepatitis Day is a World Health Organization global health campaign raising awareness about viral hepatitis and its impact on millions worldwide.
holidays.funFact: Viral hepatitis infects approximately 354 million people worldwide and causes more than 1.1 million deaths per year, yet it remains one of the most underfunded infectious diseases.
holidays.shareText: Raising awareness about viral hepatitis today because knowledge saves lives. 🎗️ #WorldHepatitisDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Chicken Wing Day
holidays.description: National Chicken Wing Day celebrates the beloved appetizer and snack food that has become a staple of American sports bar culture.
holidays.funFact: The Buffalo wing was invented in 1964 at the Anchor Bar in Buffalo, New York, by Teressa Bellissimo, who cooked leftover wings in hot sauce as a late night snack.
holidays.shareText: Getting saucy for National Chicken Wing Day! 🍗 #NationalChickenWingDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: NASA Day
holidays.description: NASA Day celebrates the National Aeronautics and Space Administration and its decades of groundbreaking achievements in space exploration and scientific discovery.
holidays.funFact: NASA was established on July 29, 1958, by President Dwight D. Eisenhower, and since its founding has achieved milestones including the moon landing, the Hubble Space Telescope, and the Mars rovers.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the agency that reached for the stars and touched the moon on NASA Day! 🚀 #NASADay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cheesecake Day
holidays.description: National Cheesecake Day celebrates the rich, creamy dessert with a crumbly crust that has been enjoyed since ancient Greece.
holidays.funFact: Cheesecake is believed to have originated in ancient Greece and was reportedly served to athletes at the first Olympic Games in 776 BCE as an energy booster.
holidays.shareText: Every day should be National Cheesecake Day, but today it is official! 🍰 #NationalCheesecakeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Friendship Day
holidays.description: International Friendship Day celebrates the bonds of friendship and the joy that comes from meaningful connections with people around the world.
holidays.funFact: International Friendship Day was proposed in 1958 in Paraguay and was officially recognized by the United Nations in 2011, though many countries celebrate it on different dates.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the people who make life so much better on International Friendship Day! 🤝 #InternationalFriendshipDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Mutt Day
holidays.description: National Mutt Day celebrates mixed breed dogs and encourages the adoption of these lovable, unique companions from shelters.
holidays.funFact: Mixed breed dogs, or mutts, often exhibit what is called hybrid vigor, a tendency to be healthier and live longer than purebred dogs due to their greater genetic diversity.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating every gloriously unique mixed breed pup on National Mutt Day! 🐕 #NationalMuttDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Uncommon Instrument Awareness Day
holidays.description: Uncommon Instrument Awareness Day celebrates the weird, wonderful, and rarely heard musical instruments that exist beyond the standard orchestra.
holidays.funFact: Some of the world's most unusual instruments include the theremin, which is played without touching it, and the glass armonica, invented by Benjamin Franklin in 1761.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the wonderfully weird world of unusual instruments today! 🎵 #UncommonInstrumentAwarenessDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Girlfriends Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate and appreciate the special girlfriends in your life.
holidays.funFact: Friendships between women have been shown to reduce stress and increase longevity.
holidays.shareText: Shoutout to all my amazing girlfriends today! 💕 #NationalGirlfiendsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Raspberry Cream Pie Day
holidays.description: A delicious holiday dedicated to the sweet and tangy raspberry cream pie.
holidays.funFact: Raspberries are one of the few fruits that are harvested twice a year in mild climates.
holidays.shareText: Slice into something sweet today for National Raspberry Cream Pie Day! #RaspberryCreamPieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Ice Cream Sandwich Day
holidays.description: A celebration of the beloved frozen treat sandwiched between two cookies or wafers.
holidays.funFact: The ice cream sandwich was reportedly first sold for one penny by a street vendor in New York City in 1899.
holidays.shareText: Nothing beats an ice cream sandwich on a hot summer day! #IceCreamSandwichDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Coloring Book Day
holidays.description: A day to pick up some colored pencils and enjoy the relaxing art of coloring.
holidays.funFact: Adult coloring books became a massive trend in 2015, with millions of copies sold worldwide.
holidays.shareText: Color your world bright today! 🎨 #NationalColoringBookDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Watermelon Day
holidays.description: A juicy celebration of one of summer's most iconic fruits.
holidays.funFact: Watermelons are composed of about 92 percent water, which is how they got their name.
holidays.shareText: Summer is not complete without a slice of watermelon! 🍉 #NationalWatermelonDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Grab Some Nuts Day
holidays.description: A fun day encouraging people to enjoy nuts as a healthy and satisfying snack.
holidays.funFact: Peanuts are technically legumes, not true nuts, but they are one of the most popular nut snacks in the world.
holidays.shareText: Going nuts for nuts today! 🥜 #GrabSomeNutsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Chocolate Chip Cookie Day
holidays.description: A sweet celebration of America's favorite cookie loaded with chocolate chips.
holidays.funFact: Ruth Wakefield invented the chocolate chip cookie in 1938 at the Toll House Inn in Massachusetts.
holidays.shareText: Warm cookies fresh from the oven today! 🍪 #ChocolateChipCookieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Coast Guard Day
holidays.description: A day honoring the United States Coast Guard and its brave service members.
holidays.funFact: The United States Coast Guard was established on August 4, 1790, making it one of the oldest branches of the armed forces.
holidays.shareText: Honoring the brave men and women of the Coast Guard today! ⚓ #CoastGuardDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Oyster Day
holidays.description: A day to savor one of the ocean's most prized delicacies.
holidays.funFact: Oysters are filter feeders and a single oyster can filter up to 50 gallons of water per day.
holidays.shareText: Shucking and slurping in honor of National Oyster Day! 🦪 #NationalOysterDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Underwear Day
holidays.description: A lighthearted day celebrating comfort, confidence, and the unsung hero of your wardrobe.
holidays.funFact: The average American owns about 20 pairs of underwear according to consumer surveys.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the most important layer today! 😄 #NationalUnderwearDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Root Beer Float Day
holidays.description: A bubbly celebration of the classic American treat combining root beer and vanilla ice cream.
holidays.funFact: The root beer float, also called a Black Cow, was invented by Frank Wisner in Colorado in 1893.
holidays.shareText: Fizzy, creamy, and totally delicious! Happy National Root Beer Float Day! #RootBeerFloatDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Wiggle Your Toes Day
holidays.description: A playful reminder to kick off your shoes and enjoy a little foot freedom.
holidays.funFact: Each human foot has 26 bones, 33 joints, and more than 100 muscles, tendons, and ligaments.
holidays.shareText: Kicking off my shoes for Wiggle Your Toes Day! 🦶 #WiggleYourToesDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Lighthouse Day
holidays.description: A day to appreciate the historic and iconic structures that have guided sailors safely to shore.
holidays.funFact: The oldest lighthouse in the United States still in use is the Boston Light, first lit in 1716.
holidays.shareText: Standing tall and shining bright! Happy National Lighthouse Day! #NationalLighthouseDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Professional Speakers Day
holidays.description: A day recognizing the talented speakers who inspire, educate, and entertain audiences worldwide.
holidays.funFact: Public speaking is consistently ranked as one of the top fears among Americans, above even the fear of death.
holidays.shareText: Raising a mic to all the amazing speakers out there! 🎤 #ProfessionalSpeakersDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Dollar Day
holidays.description: A day marking the anniversary of the establishment of the United States monetary system.
holidays.funFact: The U.S. monetary system was established by Congress on August 8, 1786, adopting the dollar as the standard unit of currency.
holidays.shareText: Every dollar has a story! Happy National Dollar Day! #NationalDollarDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Zucchini Day
holidays.description: A celebration of the prolific summer squash that overtakes every garden in August.
holidays.funFact: Zucchini plants are so productive that gardeners often leave bags of zucchini on neighbors doorsteps anonymously.
holidays.shareText: Zucchini everything today! 🥒 #ZucchiniDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Rice Pudding Day
holidays.description: A comforting celebration of the creamy, sweet dessert enjoyed across many cultures.
holidays.funFact: Rice pudding is one of the oldest known desserts, with recipes dating back over 2,000 years to ancient China and India.
holidays.shareText: Creamy, dreamy rice pudding deserves its own day! #NationalRicePuddingDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Book Lovers Day
holidays.description: A perfect day to curl up with a great book and celebrate the joy of reading.
holidays.funFact: The world's largest library, the Library of Congress, holds over 170 million items in its collections.
holidays.shareText: Lost in a good book today and loving it! 📚 #NationalBookLoversDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Smores Day
holidays.description: A campfire classic celebration of the gooey, chocolatey, graham cracker treat.
holidays.funFact: The first published recipe for smores appeared in a Girl Scout handbook in 1927 under the name Some Mores.
holidays.shareText: Gather around the fire for National Smores Day! 🔥 #NationalSmoresDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Lazy Day
holidays.description: An official excuse to do absolutely nothing and enjoy some well deserved rest.
holidays.funFact: Studies show that taking rest days and allowing yourself to be idle can actually boost creativity and productivity.
holidays.shareText: Doing absolutely nothing today and proud of it! 😴 #NationalLazyDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Presidential Joke Day
holidays.description: A lighthearted day commemorating a famous self deprecating joke made by President Ronald Reagan.
holidays.funFact: On August 11, 1984, President Reagan joked into a live microphone that he had outlawed Russia and bombing would begin in five minutes.
holidays.shareText: Even presidents need a good laugh! Happy National Presidential Joke Day! #PresidentialJokeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Raspberry Bombe Day
holidays.description: A day dedicated to the elegant dome shaped frozen dessert layered with raspberry flavors.
holidays.funFact: The bombe glacee dessert originated in France in the late 19th century and was named for its cannonball shape.
holidays.shareText: Fancy frozen desserts for everyone today! 🍨 #NationalRaspberryBombeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Middle Child Day
holidays.description: A special day to recognize and celebrate the often overlooked middle children of the family.
holidays.funFact: Famous middle children include Bill Gates, Abraham Lincoln, and Princess Diana.
holidays.shareText: To every middle child out there, today is YOUR day! 🧒 #NationalMiddleChildDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Elephant Day
holidays.description: A global day to raise awareness about the protection and preservation of the world's elephants.
holidays.funFact: Elephants are the only animals that cannot jump, and they are also one of the few animals that can recognize themselves in a mirror.
holidays.shareText: Protecting the gentle giants of our world! 🐘 #WorldElephantDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Left Handers Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the uniqueness of left handed people and raising awareness of their challenges in a right handed world.
holidays.funFact: Only about 10 percent of the world's population is left handed, yet many famous geniuses including Leonardo da Vinci and Albert Einstein were left handed.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating all the lefties out there today! ✋ #LeftHandersDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Filet Mignon Day
holidays.description: A luxurious day dedicated to the most tender and prized cut of beef.
holidays.funFact: Filet mignon comes from the small end of the tenderloin and each cow only yields about 4 to 6 pounds of this coveted cut.
holidays.shareText: Treating myself to the finest cut tonight! 🥩 #NationalFiletMignonDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Creamsicle Day
holidays.description: A nostalgic celebration of the classic orange and vanilla ice cream bar on a stick.
holidays.funFact: The Creamsicle was invented in 1905 by 11 year old Frank Epperson, who accidentally left a fruit drink with a stick in it outside on a cold night.
holidays.shareText: That orange and cream combo is pure summer magic! #NationalCreamsicleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Financial Awareness Day
holidays.description: A day encouraging people to review their finances and improve their financial literacy.
holidays.funFact: Only about 57 percent of American adults are considered financially literate according to the FINRA Investor Education Foundation.
holidays.shareText: Taking control of my finances today! 💰 #FinancialAwarenessDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Relaxation Day
holidays.description: A day dedicated to slowing down, de stressing, and taking care of your mental well being.
holidays.funFact: Regular relaxation can lower blood pressure, reduce muscle tension, and improve concentration and mood.
holidays.shareText: Pressing pause on the chaos today! 🧘 #NationalRelaxationDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Lemon Meringue Pie Day
holidays.description: A tart and sweet celebration of the classic pie topped with fluffy toasted meringue.
holidays.funFact: Meringue was invented in the 17th century by a Swiss pastry chef named Gasparini in the town of Meiringen.
holidays.shareText: Tangy, sweet, and absolutely dreamy! Happy National Lemon Meringue Pie Day! #LemonMeringuePieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Tell a Joke Day
holidays.description: A fun filled day encouraging everyone to share their best jokes and spread laughter.
holidays.funFact: Laughter has been scientifically proven to boost the immune system, relieve pain, and reduce stress hormones.
holidays.shareText: Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything! Happy National Tell a Joke Day! #TellAJokeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Rum Day
holidays.description: A tropical celebration of one of the world's oldest and most beloved spirits.
holidays.funFact: Rum was the most popular distilled spirit in colonial America, and the first rum distillery in the colonies was established on Staten Island in 1664.
holidays.shareText: Raising a glass to National Rum Day! 🍹 #NationalRumDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Vanilla Custard Day
holidays.description: A smooth and creamy celebration of the classic vanilla custard dessert.
holidays.funFact: Vanilla is the second most expensive spice in the world after saffron, due to the labor intensive process of pollinating the vanilla orchid by hand.
holidays.shareText: Smooth, creamy, and simply delicious! Happy National Vanilla Custard Day! #VanillaCustardDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Honey Bee Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate and raise awareness about the essential role honey bees play in our ecosystem.
holidays.funFact: A single honey bee will produce only about one twelfth of a teaspoon of honey in its entire lifetime.
holidays.shareText: Buzzing with gratitude for our precious honey bees! 🐝 #NationalHoneyBeeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Fajita Day
holidays.description: A sizzling celebration of the beloved Tex Mex dish loaded with grilled meat and vegetables.
holidays.funFact: The word fajita comes from the Spanish word faja, meaning strip or belt, referring to the cut of skirt steak used in the original dish.
holidays.shareText: Sizzling up something spectacular for National Fajita Day! 🌮 #NationalFajitaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Bad Poetry Day
holidays.description: A wonderfully silly day that celebrates and embraces intentionally terrible poetry.
holidays.funFact: Bad poetry has a long literary tradition, and many great poets like Shakespeare intentionally wrote bad verse for comedic effect in their plays.
holidays.shareText: Roses are red, violets are blue, my poetry is bad, how about you? 😄 #BadPoetryDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Photography Day
holidays.description: A global celebration of the art, craft, and science of photography.
holidays.funFact: The world's first photograph was taken by Joseph Nicephore Niepce in 1826 and required an 8 hour exposure time.
holidays.shareText: Capturing the world one frame at a time! 📸 #WorldPhotographyDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Aviation Day
holidays.description: A day honoring the history and development of aviation, established to celebrate Orville Wright's birthday.
holidays.funFact: The Wright brothers' first flight at Kitty Hawk on December 17, 1903 lasted only 12 seconds and covered 120 feet.
holidays.shareText: From 12 seconds to supersonic! Happy National Aviation Day! ✈️ #NationalAviationDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Radio Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the invention of radio and its lasting impact on communication and entertainment.
holidays.funFact: More than 3 billion people around the world listen to the radio each week, making it still one of the most widely used communication tools.
holidays.shareText: Tuning in to celebrate National Radio Day! 📻 #NationalRadioDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Mosquito Day
holidays.description: A day commemorating the discovery that mosquitoes transmit malaria, honoring Dr. Ronald Ross's breakthrough in 1897.
holidays.funFact: Mosquitoes are considered the deadliest animals in the world, responsible for the deaths of more people than any other creature through disease transmission.
holidays.shareText: Raising awareness about mosquito borne diseases today! 🦟 #WorldMosquitoDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Senior Citizens Day
holidays.description: A day to honor, respect, and celebrate the wisdom and contributions of senior citizens.
holidays.funFact: President Ronald Reagan proclaimed August 21 as National Senior Citizens Day in 1988, recognizing the contributions of older Americans.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the wisdom and stories of our seniors today! 👴 #SeniorCitizensDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Spumoni Day
holidays.description: A colorful celebration of the Italian layered ice cream dessert traditionally made with fruit and nuts.
holidays.funFact: Spumoni originated in Naples, Italy in the 19th century and its name comes from the Italian word spuma, meaning foam or froth.
holidays.shareText: Three layers of Italian frozen delight! Happy National Spumoni Day! #NationalSpumoniDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Bao Day
holidays.description: A steamy celebration of the beloved Chinese steamed bun filled with savory or sweet ingredients.
holidays.funFact: Bao buns have been eaten in China for over 1,800 years and were reportedly invented by the strategist Zhuge Liang during the Three Kingdoms period.
holidays.shareText: Soft, fluffy, and absolutely stuffed with flavor! Happy National Bao Day! #NationalBaoDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Eat a Peach Day
holidays.description: A juicy celebration of one of summer's most beloved and fragrant fruits.
holidays.funFact: Peaches are native to China and have been cultivated there for over 4,000 years, where they symbolize immortality and good luck.
holidays.shareText: Biting into summer's sweetest fruit today! 🍑 #NationalEatAPeachDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Sponge Cake Day
holidays.description: A light and airy celebration of the delicate sponge cake that has been delighting sweet tooths for centuries.
holidays.funFact: Sponge cake is one of the oldest known non yeast cakes, with recipes appearing in English cookbooks as far back as the 15th century.
holidays.shareText: Light, fluffy, and absolutely heavenly! Happy National Sponge Cake Day! #SpongeCakeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Ride the Wind Day
holidays.description: A breezy day celebrating the joy of outdoor activities powered by the wind, like kite flying and sailing.
holidays.funFact: The earliest kites were invented in China approximately 2,800 years ago and were used for military signaling and measuring distances.
holidays.shareText: Letting the wind take me somewhere wonderful today! #RideTheWindDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Waffle Day
holidays.description: A crispy and golden celebration of the beloved grid patterned breakfast treat.
holidays.funFact: The first U.S. patent for a waffle iron was issued to Cornelius Swarthout of Troy, New York on August 24, 1869.
holidays.shareText: Syrup on everything today! Happy National Waffle Day! 🧇 #NationalWaffleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Peach Pie Day
holidays.description: A sweet and flaky celebration of the summertime classic peach pie.
holidays.funFact: Georgia is nicknamed the Peach State, though California actually produces the most peaches in the United States.
holidays.shareText: Nothing says summer like a fresh peach pie! 🥧 #NationalPeachPieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Banana Split Day
holidays.description: A festive celebration of the classic ice cream sundae made with a split banana and three scoops.
holidays.funFact: The banana split was invented by 23 year old David Strickler of Latrobe, Pennsylvania in 1904 and sold for a whopping 10 cents.
holidays.shareText: Three scoops and a whole lot of fun! Happy National Banana Split Day! #BananaSplitDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Whiskey Sour Day
holidays.description: A tart and smooth celebration of the classic cocktail combining whiskey, lemon juice, and simple syrup.
holidays.funFact: The whiskey sour is one of the oldest cocktails in existence, with the first published recipe appearing in Jerry Thomas's 1862 bartender's guide.
holidays.shareText: Perfectly balanced and wonderfully tart! Happy National Whiskey Sour Day! #WhiskeySourDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Dog Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate all dogs and encourage adoption from shelters and rescues.
holidays.funFact: Dogs have been human companions for over 15,000 years, making them the first domesticated animal in human history.
holidays.shareText: Every day is better with a dog in it! 🐶 #NationalDogDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cherry Popsicle Day
holidays.description: A cool and sweet celebration of the classic cherry flavored frozen treat on a stick.
holidays.funFact: The popsicle was accidentally invented in 1905 by 11 year old Frank Epperson when he left a mix of water and powdered soda with a stirring stick outside overnight.
holidays.shareText: Cherry red and ice cold perfection! Happy National Cherry Popsicle Day! #CherryPopsicleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pots de Creme Day
holidays.description: A luxurious celebration of the rich French custard dessert baked in small individual pots.
holidays.funFact: Pots de creme translates to pots of cream in French and dates back to 17th century French cuisine as a decadent royal dessert.
holidays.shareText: Ooh la la! Indulging in some rich pots de creme today! #PotsDeCremeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Just Because Day
holidays.description: A spontaneous day encouraging you to do something nice or surprising for absolutely no reason at all.
holidays.funFact: Random acts of kindness have been shown to increase serotonin levels in both the giver and the receiver, boosting happiness for everyone involved.
holidays.shareText: Doing something spontaneous today, just because I can! 🎉 #NationalJustBecauseDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Bow Tie Day
holidays.description: A dapper day celebrating the stylish and distinguished bow tie and those who wear it with flair.
holidays.funFact: The bow tie originated in Croatia in the 17th century and was adopted by Parisian fashion, eventually becoming a symbol of intellectual sophistication.
holidays.shareText: Bow ties are cool! Happy National Bow Tie Day! 🎀 #NationalBowTieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Race Your Mouse Around the Icons Day
holidays.description: A quirky and playful tech holiday celebrating the fun of clicking and moving your computer mouse.
holidays.funFact: The computer mouse was invented by Doug Engelbart in 1964 at the Stanford Research Institute and was originally a wooden shell with two metal wheels.
holidays.shareText: On your marks, get set, click! Happy Race Your Mouse Around the Icons Day! 🖱️ #MouseRaceDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Chop Suey Day
holidays.description: A savory celebration of the popular stir fried noodle and vegetable dish with Chinese American roots.
holidays.funFact: Chop suey was likely invented in the United States by Chinese immigrants in the late 19th century and the name roughly translates to mixed pieces in Cantonese.
holidays.shareText: Stir frying up something delicious today! 🍜 #NationalChopSueyDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: More Herbs Less Salt Day
holidays.description: A health conscious day encouraging people to flavor their food with fresh herbs instead of salt.
holidays.funFact: Replacing salt with herbs like rosemary, thyme, and basil can reduce sodium intake while actually increasing the antioxidant content of your meals.
holidays.shareText: Swapping the salt shaker for fresh herbs today! 🌿 #MoreHerbsLessSaltDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Toasted Marshmallow Day
holidays.description: A golden and gooey celebration of the perfect campfire treat, toasted to perfection.
holidays.funFact: The perfect toasted marshmallow is achieved at around 300 degrees Fahrenheit, when the sugar on the outside caramelizes into a crispy golden shell.
holidays.shareText: Golden on the outside, gooey on the inside! Happy National Toasted Marshmallow Day! #ToastedMarshmallowDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Grief Awareness Day
holidays.description: A compassionate day dedicated to raising awareness about grief and encouraging open conversations about loss.
holidays.funFact: Grief is not just an emotional experience but also a physical one, and research shows that grief can temporarily weaken the immune system.
holidays.shareText: Today we honor those we have lost and support those who are grieving. 💙 #GriefAwarenessDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Trail Mix Day
holidays.description: A crunchy celebration of the portable and nutritious snack beloved by hikers and snackers alike.
holidays.funFact: Trail mix became popular in the 1970s as part of the outdoor recreation boom, though similar combinations of nuts and dried fruit have been eaten by travelers for centuries.
holidays.shareText: Hitting the trail with the perfect snack in hand! 🥾 #NationalTrailMixDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Matchmaker Day
holidays.description: A romantic day celebrating the art of matchmaking and the joy of helping people find meaningful connections.
holidays.funFact: Professional matchmaking is one of the oldest professions in the world, with records of matchmakers in ancient China dating back over 2,000 years.
holidays.shareText: Love is in the air on the last day of August! 💞 #NationalMatchmakerDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Gyro Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate the delicious Greek sandwich filled with seasoned meat, fresh veggies, and creamy tzatziki sauce.
holidays.funFact: The word gyro comes from the Greek word for turn, referring to the rotating spit the meat is cooked on.
holidays.shareText: Spinning into September with a delicious gyro! Happy National Gyro Day! #NationalGyroDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Letter Writing Day
holidays.description: A day dedicated to the timeless art of writing personal letters and reconnecting with loved ones through handwritten words.
holidays.funFact: Before email existed, the average person sent around 40 personal letters per year.
holidays.shareText: Put pen to paper today and make someones day! Happy World Letter Writing Day! #WorldLetterWritingDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National VJ Day
holidays.description: Commemorating Victory over Japan Day, marking the end of World War II and honoring those who served.
holidays.funFact: VJ Day on September 2, 1945 marked the formal surrender of Japan aboard the USS Missouri in Tokyo Bay.
holidays.shareText: Remembering and honoring the end of World War II on National VJ Day. #NationalVJDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Coconut Day
holidays.description: A global celebration of the coconut, one of the most versatile and nutritious fruits found in tropical regions worldwide.
holidays.funFact: Coconut water was used as an emergency intravenous hydration fluid during World War II when medical saline was scarce.
holidays.shareText: Going nuts for coconuts today! Happy World Coconut Day! #WorldCoconutDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Welsh Rarebit Day
holidays.description: A day to enjoy this classic British dish of a savory cheese sauce served hot over toasted bread.
holidays.funFact: Despite the name, Welsh Rarebit contains no rabbit. The dish was historically a cheap alternative to meat for Welsh people.
holidays.shareText: Toasting to a cheesy classic on National Welsh Rarebit Day! #NationalWelshRarebitDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Skyscraper Day
holidays.description: A day to marvel at the engineering feats of the worlds tallest buildings and the architects who designed them.
holidays.funFact: The first skyscraper was the Home Insurance Building in Chicago, built in 1885, standing just 10 stories tall.
holidays.shareText: Looking up and dreaming big on Skyscraper Day! #SkyscraperDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Macadamia Nut Day
holidays.description: A day to indulge in the rich, buttery flavor of macadamia nuts, native to Australia and beloved worldwide.
holidays.funFact: Macadamia nuts are the most expensive nuts in the world, partly because the trees take 7 to 10 years to begin producing nuts.
holidays.shareText: Treating myself to the king of nuts today! Happy National Macadamia Nut Day! #NationalMacadamiaNutDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Newspaper Carrier Day
holidays.description: A day honoring the hardworking newspaper carriers who deliver the news rain or shine to doorsteps everywhere.
holidays.funFact: The first newspaper carrier was 10 year old Barney Flaherty, hired by the New York Sun in 1833.
holidays.shareText: Shoutout to every newspaper carrier out there keeping us informed! Happy Newspaper Carrier Day! #NewspaperCarrierDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cheese Pizza Day
holidays.description: A celebration of the most classic pizza of all, simple, gooey, and perfectly cheesy with just sauce and mozzarella.
holidays.funFact: Americans consume approximately 3 billion pizzas per year, and cheese pizza remains the single most popular variety.
holidays.shareText: Keeping it classic with a perfect slice of cheese pizza today! #NationalCheesePizzaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Be Late for Something Day
holidays.description: A whimsical holiday giving everyone permission to slow down and not stress about being on time just for one day.
holidays.funFact: The average American spends about 55 minutes per day waiting for things or people who are running late.
holidays.shareText: Running fashionably late today and loving every second! Happy Be Late for Something Day! #BeLateForSomethingDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Coffee Ice Cream Day
holidays.description: A day to enjoy the perfect marriage of two beloved flavors, the bold kick of coffee blended into creamy frozen ice cream.
holidays.funFact: Coffee ice cream was first commercially produced in the United States in the 1880s and has been popular ever since.
holidays.shareText: Two favorites in one glorious scoop! Happy National Coffee Ice Cream Day! #NationalCoffeeIceCreamDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Read a Book Day
holidays.description: A day encouraging everyone to pick up a book, explore new worlds, and enjoy the simple pleasure of reading.
holidays.funFact: Reading for just six minutes can reduce stress levels by up to 68 percent, more than listening to music or taking a walk.
holidays.shareText: Cracking open a good book and escaping into another world today! Happy Read a Book Day! #ReadABookDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Acorn Squash Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate the beloved acorn squash, a fall favorite with its sweet, nutty flesh and distinctive ridged shape.
holidays.funFact: Acorn squash is technically a fruit, and one cup provides over 100 percent of your daily recommended vitamin C.
holidays.shareText: Welcoming fall flavors with some delicious acorn squash! #NationalAcornSquashDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Neither Snow nor Rain Day
holidays.description: A day honoring the unofficial motto of the US Postal Service and the dedicated letter carriers who deliver no matter the weather.
holidays.funFact: The famous phrase neither snow nor rain is actually taken from a description of Persian postal couriers written by Herodotus around 500 BC.
holidays.shareText: Rain or shine, the mail gets through! Celebrating National Neither Snow nor Rain Day! #NeitherSnowNorRainDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Date Nut Bread Day
holidays.description: A day to bake and enjoy a hearty loaf of date nut bread, a beloved classic packed with sweet dates and crunchy walnuts.
holidays.funFact: Date palms are one of the oldest cultivated trees in the world, with evidence of date farming dating back over 7,000 years.
holidays.shareText: Slicing into a warm loaf of date nut bread today! Happy National Date Nut Bread Day! #NationalDateNutBreadDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Literacy Day
holidays.description: A UNESCO designated day highlighting the importance of literacy as a matter of dignity and human rights around the world.
holidays.funFact: Today about 773 million adults worldwide lack basic literacy skills, and two thirds of them are women.
holidays.shareText: Every person deserves the power of literacy. Celebrating International Literacy Day! #InternationalLiteracyDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Teddy Bear Day
holidays.description: A cuddly holiday celebrating the beloved stuffed toy that has brought comfort and joy to children and adults for over a century.
holidays.funFact: The teddy bear was named after President Theodore Roosevelt, who famously refused to shoot a bear that had been tied to a tree during a hunt in 1902.
holidays.shareText: Giving my favorite stuffed companion a big squeeze today! Happy National Teddy Bear Day! #NationalTeddyBearDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Wiener Schnitzel Day
holidays.description: A day to enjoy Austria's most famous dish, a thin, breaded, and pan fried veal or pork cutlet served with lemon.
holidays.funFact: Authentic Wiener Schnitzel must be made with veal according to Austrian law. Versions made with pork must be labeled Schnitzel Wiener Art.
holidays.shareText: Pounding, breading, and frying up something delicious! Happy National Wiener Schnitzel Day! #NationalWienerSchnitzelDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National TV Dinner Day
holidays.description: A nostalgic celebration of the frozen TV dinner, the iconic convenience food that changed American eating habits in the 1950s.
holidays.funFact: The first Swanson TV dinner was sold in 1953 for 98 cents and contained turkey with cornbread dressing, sweet potatoes, and peas.
holidays.shareText: Kicking back with a classic TV dinner tonight! Happy National TV Dinner Day! #NationalTVDinnerDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Suicide Prevention Day
holidays.description: A global day of awareness organized to provide commitment and action to prevent suicide and support those affected by it.
holidays.funFact: Research shows that asking someone directly about suicide does not increase risk and can actually provide relief and open doors to help.
holidays.shareText: You are not alone. Reach out, listen, and be there for someone today. #WorldSuicidePreventionDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Patriot Day
holidays.description: A solemn day of remembrance honoring the nearly 3,000 people who lost their lives in the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks.
holidays.funFact: Congress designated September 11 as Patriot Day in 2001, and flags are flown at half staff across the country on this day.
holidays.shareText: Never forgetting. Honoring all those lost and the heroes who responded. #PatriotDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Hot Cross Bun Day
holidays.description: A day to enjoy the sweet, spiced buns marked with a cross on top, traditionally eaten on Good Friday but beloved year round.
holidays.funFact: Hot cross buns have been made since at least the 12th century, when a monk at St Albans Abbey is said to have distributed them to the poor.
holidays.shareText: One a penny, two a penny, hot cross buns! Celebrating National Hot Cross Bun Day! #NationalHotCrossBunDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Chocolate Milkshake Day
holidays.description: A delicious day to celebrate the thick, creamy, chocolate milkshake, one of Americas most beloved frozen treats.
holidays.funFact: The first chocolate milkshake recipe appeared in 1885, but it contained whiskey. The non alcoholic version became popular in the early 1900s.
holidays.shareText: Sipping on the most perfect chocolatey creation today! Happy National Chocolate Milkshake Day! #NationalChocolateMilkshakeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Video Games Day
holidays.description: A day for gamers everywhere to celebrate the art, culture, and pure joy of video games across all platforms and genres.
holidays.funFact: The video game industry generates more revenue annually than the film and music industries combined.
holidays.shareText: Leveling up and celebrating the games we love! Happy National Video Games Day! #NationalVideoGamesDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Peanut Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate the humble peanut, technically a legume and the star ingredient in countless foods from peanut butter to trail mix.
holidays.funFact: It takes about 540 peanuts to make one 12 ounce jar of peanut butter.
holidays.shareText: Going nuts for peanuts today! Happy National Peanut Day! #NationalPeanutDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Programmers Day
holidays.description: A day celebrated on the 256th day of the year honoring software developers and the code that powers our modern world.
holidays.funFact: The number 256 was chosen because it is the highest power of two that is less than 365, representing the number of distinct values in one byte.
holidays.shareText: Here is to the developers turning coffee into code! Happy International Programmers Day! #InternationalProgrammersDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cream Filled Donut Day
holidays.description: A decadent day celebrating the beloved cream filled donut, pillowy fried dough stuffed with rich, velvety cream.
holidays.funFact: The cream filled donut is thought to have originated in Germany, where Bismarck donuts filled with jam or cream have been popular for centuries.
holidays.shareText: Life is better with cream filled donuts. Happy National Cream Filled Donut Day! #NationalCreamFilledDonutDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Eat a Hoagie Day
holidays.description: A day to dig into a classic hoagie, the Philadelphia style submarine sandwich piled high with meats, cheese, and fresh toppings.
holidays.funFact: The hoagie is believed to have originated in Philadelphia, where workers at the Hog Island shipyard were said to eat large meat sandwiches for lunch.
holidays.shareText: Stacking it high and eating it proud on Eat a Hoagie Day! #EatAHogieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Linguine Day
holidays.description: A day to twirl up a fork of linguine, the flat, narrow pasta that is perfect with clam sauce, pesto, or any favorite topping.
holidays.funFact: Linguine, meaning little tongues in Italian, originated in the Liguria region of Italy and is the traditional pasta for dishes like linguine alle vongole.
holidays.shareText: Twirling into the perfect pasta day! Happy National Linguine Day! #NationalLinguineDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Day of Democracy
holidays.description: A United Nations day to promote and uphold the principles of democracy and the importance of civic participation worldwide.
holidays.funFact: As of today, about half of the worlds countries hold multiparty elections, yet only about 12 percent are considered full democracies.
holidays.shareText: Democracy is not a spectator sport. Engage, vote, and participate! #InternationalDayOfDemocracy #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Guacamole Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate the creamy, green perfection of guacamole, the beloved avocado based dip with roots in Aztec cuisine.
holidays.funFact: Americans consume over 8 million pounds of guacamole on Super Bowl Sunday alone, enough to cover an entire football field 20 feet deep.
holidays.shareText: Any day with guacamole is a great day! Happy National Guacamole Day! #NationalGuacamoleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Collect Rocks Day
holidays.description: A day encouraging geology enthusiasts and curious minds of all ages to get outside and start or add to a rock collection.
holidays.funFact: The oldest rocks ever found on Earth are the Acasta Gneisses in Canada, estimated to be about 4.03 billion years old.
holidays.shareText: Taking my geology curiosity outside today! Happy Collect Rocks Day! #CollectRocksDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Monte Cristo Sandwich Day
holidays.description: A day for the indulgent Monte Cristo, a sandwich of ham, turkey, and cheese that is battered and fried to golden perfection.
holidays.funFact: The Monte Cristo sandwich is believed to be an American variation of the French Croque Monsieur, popularized at California restaurants in the 1950s.
holidays.shareText: Savoring every fried, powdered sugar dusted bite! Happy National Monte Cristo Sandwich Day! #NationalMonteCristoSandwichDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Constitution Day
holidays.description: A day commemorating the signing of the United States Constitution on September 17, 1787, the foundation of American government.
holidays.funFact: The Constitution was written in just 100 days during the hot Philadelphia summer of 1787 and has only been amended 27 times in over 230 years.
holidays.shareText: We the People celebrate Constitution Day! #ConstitutionDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cheeseburger Day
holidays.description: A meaty celebration of one of Americas favorite foods, the classic cheeseburger with its juicy patty and melted cheese.
holidays.funFact: The cheeseburger was reportedly invented in 1926 by a 16 year old named Lionel Sternberger who added a slice of cheese to a hamburger at his fathers California diner.
holidays.shareText: Flipping and stacking the perfect cheeseburger today! Happy National Cheeseburger Day! #NationalCheeseburgerDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Water Monitoring Day
holidays.description: A global educational outreach program that builds public awareness and involvement in protecting water resources worldwide.
holidays.funFact: Only about 3 percent of the Earths water is fresh water, and less than 1 percent of that is accessible for human use.
holidays.shareText: Every drop counts. Protect our water today and every day! #WorldWaterMonitoringDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Butterscotch Pudding Day
holidays.description: A day to enjoy the warm, buttery sweetness of butterscotch pudding, a timeless dessert that brings comfort to every spoonful.
holidays.funFact: Butterscotch gets its name from the process of scotching, an old word meaning to cut or score, referring to how the candy was traditionally cut into pieces.
holidays.shareText: Indulging in a bowl of golden butterscotch perfection! Happy National Butterscotch Pudding Day! #NationalButterscotchPuddingDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Talk Like a Pirate Day
holidays.description: A fun and silly holiday encouraging everyone to sprinkle their conversations with pirate lingo, arrs, and shiver me timbers.
holidays.funFact: International Talk Like a Pirate Day was created in 1995 as a joke by two friends and became globally popular after humor columnist Dave Barry wrote about it in 2002.
holidays.shareText: Arrr, shiver me timbers! It be Talk Like a Pirate Day! #TalkLikeAPirateDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pepperoni Pizza Day
holidays.description: A day dedicated to Americas most popular pizza topping, the bold and spicy pepperoni that crowns a perfect pizza pie.
holidays.funFact: Pepperoni is the most popular pizza topping in the United States, appearing on about 36 percent of all pizzas ordered.
holidays.shareText: Piling on the pepperoni today! Happy National Pepperoni Pizza Day! #NationalPepperoniPizzaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National String Cheese Day
holidays.description: A fun day celebrating string cheese, the snackable, peel able dairy treat loved by kids and adults alike.
holidays.funFact: String cheese gets its distinctive stringy texture from the way the mozzarella is heated and stretched, aligning the proteins in one direction.
holidays.shareText: Peeling and snacking on string cheese the only correct way! Happy National String Cheese Day! #NationalStringCheeseDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Gratitude Day
holidays.description: A day to pause, reflect, and express genuine appreciation for the people, experiences, and blessings in your life.
holidays.funFact: Studies show that regularly practicing gratitude can increase happiness by up to 25 percent and improve sleep quality.
holidays.shareText: Taking a moment to count my blessings and share some gratitude today! #WorldGratitudeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pecan Cookie Day
holidays.description: A sweet day to bake and enjoy pecan cookies, featuring the rich, buttery southern nut nestled in chewy, golden cookie dough.
holidays.funFact: Pecans are the only major tree nut that is native to North America, and the United States produces about 80 percent of the worlds supply.
holidays.shareText: Baking up a batch of buttery pecan perfection! Happy National Pecan Cookie Day! #NationalPecanCookieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National White Chocolate Day
holidays.description: A day to savor white chocolate, the creamy, sweet confection made from cocoa butter without the cocoa solids of traditional chocolate.
holidays.funFact: White chocolate is technically not real chocolate according to the FDA because it contains no cocoa solids, only cocoa butter, sugar, and milk.
holidays.shareText: Letting white chocolate steal the spotlight today! Happy National White Chocolate Day! #NationalWhiteChocolateDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Business Womens Day
holidays.description: A day honoring the achievements, leadership, and contributions of women in business and entrepreneurship around the world.
holidays.funFact: Women owned businesses in the United States generate over 1.8 trillion dollars in revenue annually and employ nearly 10 million people.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the brilliant women leading and building businesses every day! #BusinessWomensDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Great American Pot Pie Day
holidays.description: A cozy day to celebrate the classic pot pie, a hearty comfort food filled with vegetables and meat beneath a golden flaky crust.
holidays.funFact: Pot pies have a history stretching back to ancient Rome, where they were considered a luxury dish served at banquets and filled with oysters and mussels.
holidays.shareText: Nothing says comfort food like a golden, flaky pot pie! Happy National Great American Pot Pie Day! #NationalPotPieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: First Day of Autumn
holidays.description: The autumnal equinox marks the official start of fall in the Northern Hemisphere, bringing cooler days, colorful leaves, and cozy vibes.
holidays.funFact: On the autumnal equinox, the sun rises due east and sets due west, and day and night are nearly equal in length all over the world.
holidays.shareText: Sweater weather, pumpkins, and falling leaves are finally here! Happy First Day of Autumn! #FirstDayOfAutumn #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cherries Jubilee Day
holidays.description: A day to enjoy cherries jubilee, the classic flambeed dessert of cherries in a warm brandy sauce served over vanilla ice cream.
holidays.funFact: Cherries jubilee was created by the legendary French chef Auguste Escoffier in honor of Queen Victorias Diamond Jubilee in 1897.
holidays.shareText: Lighting up dessert time with a classic cherries jubilee! Happy National Cherries Jubilee Day! #NationalCherriesJubileeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Punctuation Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the importance of proper punctuation and its role in making written communication clear, precise, and effective.
holidays.funFact: The most commonly misused punctuation mark is the apostrophe, which is frequently confused for possession versus contractions.
holidays.shareText: Lets eat Grandma vs. Lets eat, Grandma. Punctuation saves lives! Happy National Punctuation Day! #NationalPunctuationDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Lobster Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate the king of seafood, the magnificent lobster, enjoyed steamed, grilled, or as the star of a rich bisque.
holidays.funFact: Lobsters were once so abundant in North America that they were considered poor mans food and fed to prisoners, apprentices, and servants.
holidays.shareText: Cracking into the king of the sea today! Happy National Lobster Day! #NationalLobsterDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Dream Day
holidays.description: A global day inspiring people everywhere to pursue their dreams, share their visions, and take action toward a better future.
holidays.funFact: Research suggests that people who write down their dreams and goals are 42 percent more likely to achieve them than those who do not.
holidays.shareText: What is your dream? Today is the day to say it out loud and start chasing it! #WorldDreamDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pancake Day
holidays.description: A delicious day to celebrate the fluffy, golden, stack worthy pancake in all its delightful forms and toppings.
holidays.funFact: The oldest known pancake recipe dates back to ancient Greece and Rome, and pancake remains have even been found preserved in the stomach of Otzi the Iceman.
holidays.shareText: Stack them high and pour on the syrup! Happy National Pancake Day! #NationalPancakeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Lumberjack Day
holidays.description: A fun day celebrating the rugged skill and culture of lumberjacks, complete with flannel, axes, and the great outdoors.
holidays.funFact: Professional lumberjack competitions, known as timber sports, include events like log rolling, speed climbing, and ax throwing and have been held since 1872.
holidays.shareText: Channeling my inner lumberjack today! Happy Lumberjack Day! #LumberjackDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Chocolate Milk Day
holidays.description: A day to pour a tall, cold glass of chocolate milk and enjoy one of the worlds most universally beloved drinks.
holidays.funFact: Chocolate milk was invented by Irish botanist Hans Sloane in Jamaica in the 1680s, who mixed cocoa with milk and brought the recipe back to England.
holidays.shareText: Pouring a cold glass of pure childhood joy! Happy National Chocolate Milk Day! #NationalChocolateMilkDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Tourism Day
holidays.description: A United Nations day celebrating travel and tourism as a force for cultural understanding, economic growth, and global connection.
holidays.funFact: Tourism directly or indirectly accounts for about 10 percent of global GDP and supports 1 in 10 jobs worldwide.
holidays.shareText: The world is calling! Celebrate travel and exploration on World Tourism Day! #WorldTourismDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Strawberry Cream Pie Day
holidays.description: A sweet day to celebrate the gorgeous strawberry cream pie, with its buttery crust, silky cream filling, and fresh berry topping.
